articles.title: Q18a — Nutrition status
content: Does the elder show signs of undernutrition or significant weight loss? **Observation-based — no measurement required.** Look at arms, face, and how clothes fit. Ask family or elder if clothes have become loose over recent months.

ul: **Mild (1)** — some thinness or weight loss reported
**Moderate (2)** — visibly thin, clothes loose, chewing difficulty affecting intake
**Severe (3)** — visibly wasted, unable to chew most foods, recent rapid weight loss — **CRITICAL FLAG**

content: Severe wasting is visible without any measurement. Observe before asking. Three failure modes: (1) access — meals not coming; (2) intake — meals come but can't eat (dental, dysphagia); (3) illness-driven loss (TB, untreated diabetes, malignancy). All three may produce the same observation; the response differs.

articles.title: Q18a — Operational definitions
content: Food access ≠ nutrition status. An elder receiving daily meals can still be wasting due to dental loss, chewing difficulty, or chronic disease. Observation-based per programme decision (no MUAC measurement).

head: Response
Operational definition
rows: Some thinness or weight loss reported.
Visibly thin, clothes loose, chewing difficulty affecting intake.
Visibly wasted, unable to chew most foods, recent rapid weight loss — CRITICAL FLAG.

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Soft food adaptation discussed with cook (family or kitchen); dental referral via PHC if chewing affected; nutrition supplement if available
**Critical flag — severe undernutrition.** Emergency nutrition support (dry ration kit + community kitchen daily meals); medical workup to rule out underlying illness (TB, diabetes, malignancy)

articles.title: Q18b — Food and nutritional access
content: Does the elder have regular food access?

ul: **Severe food insecurity (3)** — serious food insecurity affecting wellbeing — **CRITICAL FLAG**

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Community kitchen enrolment (priority); family negotiation if family-cooked meals are inconsistent; dry ration kit if needed in interim
**Critical flag.** Emergency ration within 48 hours; community kitchen daily; ration card pipeline (referral to Entitlements Team)

articles.title: Q19 — Ability to afford basic needs
content: Can the elder afford medicines and basic needs?

ul: **Frequent difficulty (2)** — frequent inability to manage essential expenses
**Unable to afford (3)** — severe financial inability affecting survival or treatment

head: Response
Operational definition
rows: Frequent inability to manage essential expenses.
Severe financial inability affecting survival or treatment.

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Identify which expense — food, medicine, fuel — and address: community kitchen for food; PHC/Janaushadhi for medicine; ET for pension expediting
Immediate triage of unmet essential expense; emergency support fund if available; ET for pension/widow scheme expediting; community kitchen for daily food

articles.title: Q20 — Pension and entitlement access
content: Is the elder receiving pension or eligible entitlements regularly?

ul: **Not receiving despite eligibility (3)** — eligible but not receiving benefits

content: All Q20 responses are referred to the **Entitlements Team** (separate from elderly team). CO's job is clean handover + tracking. Track to the **money in the account**, not to 'approved.'

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: ET referred to follow up specific scheme (which pension/which card); CO tracks resolution at next visit
Priority referral to ET with full documentation of the specific entitlement and known blocker; expected resolution timeline tracked
